headline: Cuba's crackdown on dissidents continues, despite US pressure
description: In an effort to show good faith to Washington, Havana recently pardoned more than 2,000 detainees. However, most of those who benefited were ordinary delinquents. The Cuban authorities continue to arrest dissidents, bringing the number of political prisoners to 1,260, a record high.

headline: French culture minister slams Canal+ for 'disproportionate' response to open letter
Putin meets with Xi to strengthen deeply unbalanced relations
Ebola outbreak in the DRC: West's aid cuts 'contributed to the weakening of the surveillance chain'
description: In an interview, the head of Africa CDC, the African Union's health agency, denounces the impact that drastic reductions in international aid have had on the delayed management of the ongoing outbreak.

headline: San Diego mosque shooters met online and left writings expressing hate, FBI says
description: Ahead of the Islamic Center attack, where two teens killed three people on Monday, the mother of one teen reported that her son was suicidal and had run away, according to police, who said weapons and her vehicle were missing from the family's home. Two hours after her call, the shooting occurred.

headline: Cannes 2026: Pedro Almodovar's 'Bitter Christmas' explores writing and self-reflection
description: In his seventh film competing for the Palme d'Or, the Spanish filmmaker examines his own creative process through the character of a writer in crisis, played by Leonardo Sbaraglia.

headline: The EU's unflinching collaboration with Libya and Tunisia to contain the flow of migrants
description: 'Migrants in the Mediterranean: The mechanics of silence' (2/4). Giorgia Meloni's government, backed by the European Union, is funding, equipping and training both the Libyan and Tunisian coast guards – even though these forces have committed rights abuses – to intercept people in exile on their way to Europe.

How Le Pen's party has shifted its economic platform over 15 years
description: Ever since Marine Le Pen took control of the far-right Rassemblement National in 2011, it has struggled to present a clear economic platform, to the point of muddying its political identity and sparking questions about its plans.

headline: New book reveals Macron couple was rocked by two presidential terms
description: Between a president plagued by doubt and a first lady asserting herself, journalist Florian Tardif paints a striking portrait of the couple.

headline: Gaza's vanished past lives on through photographs accidentally rediscovered in Marseille
description: Kegham Djeghalian, a photographer and Armenian genocide survivor, settled in Gaza in 1944. He documented the Palestinian territory through portraits and reports until he died in 1981. His grandson has chosen to exhibit his images of ordinary and joyful moments in Gaza, now devastated by two years of war.

headline: Canal+ controversy undermines French cinema
With generative AI, 'humankind has created, for the first time in its history, a being that mimics it in its very essence: the ability to think, to create, to decide'
